notes: G2 richness batch — synopsis uplift for 33 games with no editorial_richness signal

payload.title: Batty Zabella
payload.synopsis: Batty Zabella is a Japan-only Game Boy action title published by Varie in 1992, casting players as a witch navigating overhead stages and dispatching enemies with magic projectiles in a format aimed at younger players.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only obscure GB title

payload.title: Chalvo 55
payload.synopsis: Chalvo 55 is a Japan-only Game Boy block-sliding puzzle game developed by Coconuts Japan in 1992, tasking players with guiding a round character through increasingly complex maze-like puzzle stages by pushing and arranging blocks.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only GB puzzle

payload.title: Cult Jump
payload.synopsis: Cult Jump is a Japan-only Game Boy action compilation published by Bandai in 1992 based on Weekly Shōnen Jump manga properties, pitting characters from popular series against each other in a cross-franchise brawling format unique to the Game Boy era.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Jump Comics GB crossover

payload.title: DT: Lords of Genomes
payload.synopsis: DT: Lords of Genomes is a Japan-only Game Boy strategy RPG released in 1994, placing players in command of mechanized units across grid-based tactical battles in a sci-fi setting with unit customization and turn-based combat sequences.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only GB strategy RPG

payload.title: Dangan GB2
payload.synopsis: Dangan GB2 is a Japan-only Game Boy overhead racing action game, the sequel to Dangan GB, tasking players with navigating compact circuits in high-speed vehicles while managing collisions and obstacles across a series of competitive stages.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only GB racing sequel

payload.title: Dead Names
payload.synopsis: Dead Names is a modern Game Boy homebrew horror title that delivers a dark narrative experience within the constraints of the original 1989 hardware, using monochrome pixel art and sound design to build atmospheric tension across its compact adventure.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — homebrew GB horror

payload.title: Carl Lewis Athletics 2000
payload.synopsis: Carl Lewis Athletics 2000 is a GBC track and field title endorsed by the Olympic sprinting legend, offering button-mashing sprint events, hurdles, and field disciplines in a compact handheld sports package targeting fans of the Summer Games genre.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — GBC licensed athletics game

payload.title: Bakushō! Ai no Gekijō
payload.synopsis: Bakushō! Ai no Gekijō is a Japan-only NES quiz and mini-game compilation based on the Fuji TV variety program of the same name, offering question-answer rounds and comedic segments drawn from the television show's format for home audiences.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only NES TV tie-in

payload.title: 100% Star
payload.synopsis: 100% Star is a Japan-only PlayStation idol simulation game published by Pony Canyon, casting players as the manager of an aspiring pop singer and guiding her career through training, event scheduling, and relationship management sequences.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only PS1 idol sim

payload.title: 19:03 Ueno Hatsu Yakou Ressha
payload.synopsis: 19:03 Ueno Hatsu Yakou Ressha is a Japan-only PlayStation mystery adventure based on the Seicho Matsumoto novel of the same name, following an investigator through a murder case tied to the famous 19:03 overnight train from Ueno station in a narrative-driven format.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only PS1 mystery VN

payload.title: Aces of the Air
payload.synopsis: Aces of the Air is a PlayStation flight combat title that places pilots in World War II aerial dogfights across European and Pacific theatres, offering mission-based campaign play with period-authentic aircraft types and accessible arcade-style flight controls.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — PS1 WWII flight combat

payload.title: Acid
payload.synopsis: Acid is a Japan-only PlayStation action game with a hallucinatory visual aesthetic, sending the player through surreal stage environments filled with abstract enemies in a format that prioritizes stylized presentation alongside its core combat mechanics.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only PS1 action

payload.title: Aero Dive
payload.synopsis: Aero Dive is a PlayStation flight simulation title developed for the Japanese market, offering cockpit-perspective missions in military aircraft across a campaign structure designed to balance the realism of simulation with the accessibility of an action game.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — PS1 flight sim Japan-only

payload.title: Ah Nanjarin
payload.synopsis: Ah Nanjarin is a Japan-only PlayStation puzzle game based on the Osaka-dialect comedy characters created by Yoshimoto Kogyo, challenging players with brainteaser scenarios tied to the humor and personalities of the popular Japanese comedy franchise.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only PS1 comedy puzzle

payload.title: Air Traffic Controller
payload.synopsis: Air Traffic Controller is a Japan-only PlayStation simulation that puts the player in the role of an airport traffic controller, managing aircraft approach, landing, and ground routing sequences in real-time across increasingly congested airspace scenarios.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — PS1 ATC simulation Japan

payload.title: Aironauts
payload.synopsis: Aironauts is a PlayStation aerial adventure game that blends exploration with combat, placing the player in a steampunk-inflected world navigated by small aircraft and requiring the management of fuel, altitude, and enemy encounters across open-ended stage maps.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — PS1 aerial adventure

payload.title: Aitakute...Your Smiles in My Heart
payload.synopsis: Aitakute...Your Smiles in My Heart is a Japan-only PlayStation visual novel romance game, following a male protagonist through branching narrative paths and relationship-building sequences with multiple female characters across a school-life drama structure.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only PS1 romance VN

payload.title: Alice on Borderlines
payload.synopsis: Alice on Borderlines is a Japan-only PlayStation adventure game that reframes the Alice in Wonderland mythology in a modern Japanese setting, guiding the protagonist through puzzle-solving and dialogue-driven encounters across its surreal narrative stages.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only PS1 adventure

payload.title: Alive
payload.synopsis: Alive is a Japan-only PlayStation action survival game developed by Data East in 1998, placing the player in a post-catastrophe environment and challenging them to scavenge resources, avoid threats, and navigate an open world while managing survival conditions.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only PS1 survival action

payload.title: All Star Racing
payload.synopsis: All Star Racing is a PlayStation kart-style racing game featuring cartoon characters competing across colorful themed circuits, offering single-race and championship modes in a family-oriented format that competes with the wave of mascot racers popular in the late 1990s.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — PS1 kart racer

payload.title: All Star Tennis '99
payload.synopsis: All Star Tennis '99 is a PlayStation tennis simulation featuring real professional players from the 1999 ATP and WTA tours, offering arcade-accessible mechanics with licensed rosters across multiple court surfaces and tournament structures.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — PS1 licensed tennis sim 1999

payload.title: All Star Tennis 2000
payload.synopsis: All Star Tennis 2000 is the follow-up PlayStation tennis title in the All Star series, updating the licensed ATP and WTA rosters with new players from the 2000 season while preserving the arcade-friendly mechanics of its predecessor across multiple tournament modes.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — PS1 licensed tennis sim 2000

payload.title: All-Star Racing 2
payload.synopsis: All-Star Racing 2 is the PlayStation sequel to the kart-racing series, expanding the track roster and character selection while maintaining the family-accessible racing format of the original, adding new power-up items and circuit designs for additional variety.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — PS1 kart racer sequel

payload.title: Alnam no Tsubasa: Shoujin no Sora no Kanata e
payload.synopsis: Alnam no Tsubasa: Shoujin no Sora no Kanata e is a Japan-only PlayStation historical flight adventure set in a fantasy feudal world, combining aerial navigation sequences with narrative-driven story beats across a campaign that blends period aesthetics with science-fiction elements.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only PS1 flight narrative

payload.title: Ancient Roman: Power of Dark Side
payload.synopsis: Ancient Roman: Power of Dark Side is a Japan-only PlayStation 3D action RPG developed by Altron, setting the player in a mythologized Roman Empire and combining sword combat with exploration across dungeon environments and a narrative centered on supernatural conflict.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only PS1 action RPG

payload.title: Animal Snap: Rescue Them 2 By 2
payload.synopsis: Animal Snap: Rescue Them 2 By 2 is a PlayStation puzzle game aimed at younger players, tasking them with photographing matching pairs of endangered animals across themed habitats in a Noah's Ark narrative wrapper that encourages wildlife awareness alongside its core tile-matching mechanics.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — PS1 children's puzzle

payload.title: Bio Evil (SEGA Mega Drive Tech Demo)
payload.synopsis: Bio Evil is an unreleased Sega Mega Drive tech demo showcasing the hardware's capabilities for horror-themed action gameplay, featuring sprite-based enemy encounters and environmental design elements that demonstrate the Genesis's visual potential in a survival horror-adjacent context.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Genesis unreleased tech demo

payload.title: Bouncers
payload.synopsis: Bouncers is an unreleased Sega Genesis side-scrolling action game that was in development in the early 1990s before being cancelled, with gameplay centered on bouncers protecting a nightclub from aggressive patrons across brawler-style combat stages that never reached retail.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Genesis cancelled brawler

payload.title: Daibōken: Saint Elmo's no Kiseki
payload.synopsis: Daibōken: Saint Elmo's no Kiseki is a Japan-only Sega Saturn action RPG from Coconuts Japan published in 1996, guiding players through a fantasy world with real-time combat and exploration mechanics in a colorful presentation aimed at the Japanese console RPG audience of the mid-1990s.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only Saturn action RPG

payload.title: Arabian Nights: Sabaku no Seirei-ō
payload.synopsis: Arabian Nights: Sabaku no Seirei-ō is a Japan-only Super Nintendo action RPG developed by Produce and published by Tecmo in 1996, sending the player through Middle Eastern-themed dungeons in a top-down action format with elemental spirit mechanics drawn from Arabian Nights mythology.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only SNES action RPG

payload.title: Battle Cross
payload.synopsis: Battle Cross is a Japan-only Super Nintendo overhead racing game published by I'Max in 1993, offering top-down circuit competition with small vehicle sprites across a series of compact tracks in a straightforward arcade racing format targeting the competitive SNES sports market.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only SNES racing game

payload.title: Battle Pinball
payload.synopsis: Battle Pinball is a Japan-only Super Nintendo pinball compilation from Banpresto published in 1993, featuring multiple themed tables based on Banpresto's licensed properties and offering standard flipper-based pinball gameplay across a variety of distinct table layouts.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only SNES pinball compilation

payload.title: Battle Soccer 2
payload.synopsis: Battle Soccer 2 is a Japan-only Super Nintendo soccer game developed by Banpresto in 1994 featuring characters from Banpresto's anime licences including Kamen Rider and Ultraman, blending licensed franchise characters with football gameplay in the cross-franchise sports tournament format the first Battle Soccer established.
payload.notes: G2 richness gap closer — Japan-only SNES licensed soccer sequel
